NOVO-B.CO.name: Novo Nordisk B
bull: GLP-1 franchise (Ozempic/Wegovy) retains dominant market share in the fast-growing obesity and diabetes segment
Next-generation pipeline (CagriSema, amycretin) offers meaningful upside if Phase 3 data is positive
Expanding into new geographies and indication areas (MASH, heart failure) diversifies revenue beyond core GLP-1
bear: Intensifying competition from Eli Lilly (tirzepatide/retatrutide) risks market share erosion and pricing pressure
US drug pricing reform and IRA negotiation risk could compress long-term margins on key products
Stock remains elevated on a forward P/E basis; any pipeline disappointment could trigger a sharp de-rating
NOVO-B.CO.summary: Novo Nordisk's GLP-1 moat is real but competion is stiffening and valuation leaves little margin for error.

MAERSK-B.CO.name: Maersk B
bull: Ongoing Red Sea/Houthi disruptions keep effective capacity tight and container spot rates structurally elevated vs. pre-2020 levels
Logistics & Services segment provides higher-margin, counter-cyclical revenue that reduces pure shipping-rate exposure
Attractive dividend yield and active buyback programme provide downside support
bear: Shipping is deeply cyclical; any Red Sea normalisation or trade-war-driven demand slump could collapse freight rates rapidly
Global trade volumes face headwinds from US-China tariff escalation and broader de-globalisation trends
Significant new vessel order book across the industry points to structural overcapacity risk into 2027
MAERSK-B.CO.summary: Maersk is fairly valued with near-term rate support but faces binary risk from geopolitical and macro developments.

DSV.CO.name: DSV
bull: Completed DB Schenker acquisition makes DSV the world's largest freight forwarder by revenue, unlocking substantial synergies
Asset-light model with strong free cash flow generation enables continued M&A and shareholder returns
Secular tailwinds from e-commerce logistics growth and supply chain nearshoring benefit a globally diversified operator
bear: DB Schenker integration carries execution risk; culture clashes and IT migrations could weigh on margins for 2-3 years
Freight forwarding volumes are highly correlated with global trade; a recession scenario hits revenues quickly
Premium valuation (P/E ~25x forward) limits upside and magnifies downside on any earnings miss
DSV.CO.summary: DSV's DB Schenker deal cements global leadership and synergy potential justifies the premium for long-term investors.
